records.ety: [OE. forgan, forgon, forgoon, AS. forgān, prop., to go past, hence, to abstain from; pref. for- + gān to go; akin to G. vergehen to pass away, to transgress. See Go, v. i.]
records.morph: [imp. Forwent; p. p. Forgone; p. pr. & vb. n. Forgoing.]

defs: To pass by; to leave. See 1st Forego.
quotes: For sith [since] I shall forgoon my liberty At your request.
And four [days] since Florimell the court forwent.

defs: to abstain from; to do without; to refrain from; to renounce; -- said of a thing already enjoyed, or of one within reach, or anticipated. See 1st forego, 2.
notes: ☞ This word in spelling has been confused with, and almost superseded by, forego to go before. Etymologically the form forgo is correct.
